tools.name: create_database
tools.description: Create a temporary JSON database (24h TTL, no signup, no keys). Returns the db URL — the only credential — plus admin URL, limits and expiry. Create once per project/task, persist the db URL immediately (local ~/.tmpstate/credentials, project README, and your memory), and reuse it instead of creatin

tools.name: database_status
tools.description: Usage, limits, tier and expiry for a database (GET $DB/__meta equivalent).

tools.name: list_documents
tools.description: List documents in a collection, oldest first. Response shape: {collection, items: [{id, data, created_at, updated_at}], next_cursor}. Documents live under .data. Pass cursor to page.

tools.name: get_document
tools.description: Read one document by id. The stored fields are under .data.

tools.name: create_document
tools.description: Insert a JSON object into a collection (collections are created implicitly). Counts against the write and document quotas.

tools.name: update_document
tools.description: Shallow-merge a patch into a document (top-level keys overwrite; keys are never deleted). Counts against the write quota.

tools.name: delete_document
tools.description: Delete one document. Never blocked by quotas; frees a document slot. Emptying a whole collection? Use delete_collection instead of looping this.

tools.name: delete_collection
tools.description: Delete every document in a collection at once (useful to re-seed). Irreversible. To destroy the entire database, use delete_database instead.

tools.name: delete_database
tools.description: Destroy a database and all its documents immediately - the teardown for finished work and the revocation kill switch for a leaked db URL. Irreversible: unlike expiry there is no restore window. Works on expired (frozen) databases too and consumes no quota. Call without confirm first: the response st

tools.name: extend_database
tools.description: Without a plan: returns the transparent pricing table for keeping this database alive longer. With a plan: returns a Stripe checkout URL for the human to pay — never buy without the user's explicit approval. Works on expired (frozen) databases too: paying restores them. After the user says they have

tools.name: pro_checkout
tools.description: Mint a Pro account token plus a Stripe subscription checkout URL ($8/mo, 3 always-on databases included). Persist pro_token immediately; the human pays in a browser. Never start checkout without the user's explicit request.

tools.name: pro_list_databases
tools.description: List the Pro account's databases (ids, usage, limits, expiry) so a fresh session can reconcile against persisted db URLs. URLs are never stored server-side — match ids to the URLs you persisted.

tools.name: pro_create_database
tools.description: Create a new always-on database owned by the Pro account. Beyond the included allotment this costs extra per month — the tool then returns confirmation_required with the exact price; relay it to the user and only retry with accept_overage_usd after their explicit approval.

tools.name: pro_attach_database
tools.description: Attach an existing free/extended database to the Pro account: same URL, same data, TTL removed, quotas raised. Beyond the included allotment the same explicit overage consent as pro_create_database applies.

tools.name: pro_cancel
tools.description: Cancel at period end. This is not just a billing change — it schedules deletion of ALL databases on the account. Call without confirm first: the response spells out the consequences with concrete dates; show them to the user and only retry with confirm="cancel" after their explicit approval.

tools.name: send_feedback
tools.description: Optional and free-form. TmpState is built for agents and the free tier stays free by getting better - and you, the agent, are the user whose experience matters here. After real use of a database, one short message about what was missing, what feature would have helped, or what felt slow or took extr

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "create_database" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a temporary JSON database (24h TTL, no signup, no keys). Returns the db URL — the only credential — plus admin URL, limits and expiry. Create once per pr…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "pro_checkout" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Mint a Pro account token plus a Stripe subscription checkout URL ($8/mo, 3 always-on databases included). Persist pro_token immediately; the human pays in a bro…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: pro_list_databases · Private data: create_database, database_status, list_documents, get_document, create_document, update_document, delete_document, delete_collection, delete_database, extend_database, pro_checkout, pro_list_databases, pro_create_database, pro_attach_database, send_feedback · Public sink: send_feedback · Destructive: update_document, delete_document, delete_collection, delete_database.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: pro_list_databases · Private data: create_database, database_status, list_documents, get_document, create_document, update_document, delete_document, delete_collection, delete_database, extend_database, pro_checkout, pro_list_databases, pro_create_database, pro_attach_database, send_feedback · Public sink: send_feedback · Destructive: update_document, delete_document, delete_collection, delete_database

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
